title: [Politoed] Battle Guide
summary: A [Water]-type [support] that triggers [drizzle] for a rain team, backed by utility moves like [encore], [toxic], and [scald].
strengths: [drizzle] sets up permanent rain on entry
75/100 dual bulk enables tank duties alongside [support]
[encore] plus [toxic] locks and chips oppo[sing] setup attackers
weaknesses: [Grass] and [Electric] weaknesses threaten from fast special attackers
Low offensive stats mean minimal direct pressure
[taunt] completely shuts down the utility plan

recommendedItems.reason: Wakes up immediately after a [rest] cycle

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[Politoed] is a [Water]-type [support] Pokemon whose entire role is built a[round] the hidden ability [drizzle].5x and halving [Fire]-type damage taken. In the Champions format, [Politoed] is the weather setter for dedicated rain teams, and its 90/75/100 defensive line gives it enough bulk to survive long enough to [support] with utility moves like [encore], [toxic], and [protect].

## Strengths
[drizzle] is the primary reason to run [Politoed]. The rain weather is a system-level buff that changes the damage calculation for every [Water] attacker on the team, and simultaneously reduces incoming [Fire] damage by half, which creates a structural advantage across the entire match. Beyond [drizzle], [Politoed]'s utility moveset ([scald] for burns, [encore] for lock-ins, [toxic] for passive damage, [protect] for stall) makes it a flexible slot that can stall out oppo[sing] [wall]s while the [Water] attackers on the back line clean up.

## Weaknesses & Answers
The 2x [Grass] and [Electric] weaknesses are exploited by almost every fast special attacker in the format, and [Politoed] is too slow to outspeed the threats that would remove it. Offensively [Politoed] is almost passive. [Politoed] is also only valuable on dedicated rain teams, which limits its slot flexibility significantly.

## Recommended Moveset
[scald] is the locked [Water] STAB because its 30 percent burn chance is a meaningful threat to physical attackers. [encore] is the utility slot for locking oppo[sing] Pokemon into setup moves or weak attacks. [toxic] covers the passive damage plan on oppo[sing] [wall]s that would otherwise outlast [Politoed]. [protect] fills the fourth slot to stall for [toxic] damage and scout switches. [ice-beam] is an alternate cove[rage] pick for [Grass] and [Dragon] attackers.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[leftovers] is the default for passive [recover]y that stacks with the rain team plan. [chesto-berry] is an alternate for [rest]-based builds that want a one-turn full heal. The ability must be [drizzle]. Tera [Water] maxes STAB damage, and Tera [Fairy] flips [Dragon] and [Dark] attacks into resists.

## Teammates & Synergy
[Politoed] is the core of a dedicated rain team. [Grass] and [Electric] answers are mandatory ([Ground] or [Fire] partners cover the main threats), and a cleaner on the back line converts the rain-boosted damage into wins. A weather [counter] on the oppo[sing] side can neutralize [Politoed]'s utility entirely, so be prepared to set rain again mid-match.

## Play Patterns
Lead [Politoed] to set rain, then start the utility rotation. Pivot out when [Grass] or [Electric] attackers are incoming, and save [Politoed]'s HP for a second rain activation later in the match. Save the Tera for an [Electric] or [Grass] move that would otherwise KO [Politoed], u[sing] it defensively to preserve the rain setter slot.
